kumbhathunanti.gram: neuter compound noun + quotation particle ti (kumbhathunaṁ + ti → kumbhathunanti by sandhi)
breakdown: Drum-sound; kumbhathuna is a kind of pot-drum.
Quotation marker; the niggahīta ṁ + ti becomes nti.

kumbhaṭṭhānakathaṁ.meaning: talk about the watering place; gossip at the well
kumbhaṭṭhānakathaṁ.summary: One item in the stock list of frivolous kinds of talk avoided by the meditator.
breakdown: Place. (The doubling ṭṭh is junction doubling.)

kumārakā.gram: masculine noun, diminutive of kumāra (nominative plural)

kuppati.meaning: to be angry; to be agitated, perturbed
breakdown: Root: to be agitated, to be angry.

kuru.meaning: do!, make! (a rare middle form of karoti)
kuru.gram: verb, imperative, rare middle form of karoti
breakdown: Root: to do, to make; middle stem kuru-.

kurute.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, middle voice of karoti
breakdown: Root: to do, to make; middle stem kuru-.

kurūrakammantā.meaning: of cruel occupation; those with a cruel profession

kusale.gram: adjective (locative singular or accusative plural of kusala)
breakdown: Wholesome, skilful, beneficial — of actions that bear happy results.
From √sad, sinking down; kusīta is one who sinks into indolence.

kuṇī.meaning: deformed; crooked (of limbs); a cripple-armed person

kālakiriyā.meaning: death; literally, the fulfilling of one's time
breakdown: Doing, performance (abstract noun related to karoti); kālakiriyā is "doing one's time", dying.

kālapamukha.meaning: with time as the foremost; time as chief

kālena.gram: masculine noun kāla in the instrumental singular, used adverbially

kāmabhogino.meaning: enjoying sensual pleasures; those who partake of sense pleasures

kāmabhogīnaṁ.summary: From a discourse to householders on the ten kinds of livelihood among those who enjoy sense pleasures.
kāmabhogīnaṁ.goenka: The lexicon notes that this form comes from a discourse of the Buddha addressing householders, explaining the ten kinds of performing one's livelihood among those who enjoy sense pleasures: prosperity could be acquired by righteous or unrighteous means; by violence or without it; used for one's own wellbeing only, for the wellbeing of others, or both; and finally whether one is attached to one's possessions or not. Summing up all these distinctions, ten kinds are described.

kāmaguṇehi.meaning: with the strands of sensual pleasure; by the five cords of sense pleasure
breakdown: Strand, cord, quality; the five kāmaguṇas are the pleasures of the five senses.

kāmakāmino.meaning: pleasure-loving; fond of and striving after sense pleasures

kāmassādavasena.meaning: on account of relishing sensual enjoyment
breakdown: On account of, by way of.

kāmasukhallikānuyogo.meaning: the practice of clinging to sensual happiness; devotion to sense indulgence
kāmasukhallikānuyogo.summary: One of the two extremes rejected in the Buddha's first discourse, the other being self-mortification.
kāmasukhallikānuyogo.goenka: In the Dhammacakkappavattanasutta the Buddha names kāmasukhallikānuyogo, addiction to indulgence in sensual pleasure, as one of the two extremes a meditator must avoid, the other being attakilamathānuyogo, addiction to self-mortification. The middle path of sīla, samādhi and paññā avoids both: one neither runs after pleasant sensations nor torments oneself, but observes whatever arises with equanimity.

kāmataṇhā.goenka: Kāmataṇhā is the first of the three kinds of taṇhā (craving) taught in the Second Noble Truth: craving for sensual pleasures, alongside bhavataṇhā (craving for becoming) and vibhavataṇhā (craving for annihilation). In practice it is observed as the mind's habit of reacting with craving to pleasant sensations; vipassanā weakens this habit by revealing the impermanence of every sensation.

kāmavitakkādīnaṁ.meaning: of thoughts of sense desire and the rest (thoughts of ill will and cruelty)

kāme.gram: noun (accusative plural or locative singular of kāma)
breakdown: Noun stem kāma: desire, object of sensual enjoyment.

kāmesu.meaning: in sensual pleasures; among sense desires

kāmesumicchācāro.meaning: wrong conduct in sensual pleasures; sexual misconduct
kāmesumicchācāro.summary: The third of the five precepts of sīla: abstaining from wrong conduct in sensual pleasures.
kāmesumicchācāro.gram: masculine noun, compound (locative kāmesu + micchācāra; nominative singular)
breakdown: In sensual pleasures (locative plural of kāma).
kāmesumicchācāro.goenka: Abstaining from kāmesumicchācāro, wrong conduct in sensual pleasures, is one of the five precepts of sīla on which all progress on the path rests. By restraining the misuse of the senses, above all sexual misconduct, the meditator stops generating fresh agitation and remorse, providing the base of moral conduct on which samādhi and paññā can be developed.

kāruññataṁ.meaning: compassion; the state of compassionateness (as in "out of compassion")
kāruññataṁ.gram: feminine abstract noun (accusative singular of kāruññatā)
breakdown: Compassion (lengthened to kāruñ- in this derivation).

kāruññataṁ out of.gram: feminine abstract noun, accusative singular (duplicate row; see kāruññataṁ)
kāruññataṁ out of.note: uncertain: this headword is a glossary-row artifact in which part of the English gloss "out of compassion" was attached to the headword kāruññataṁ; see the entry kāruññataṁ.

kārāpessāma.meaning: we will cause to be done; we will have (something) made
kārāpessāma.gram: verb, 1st person plural future, causative of karoti (kārāpeti)
breakdown: Causative suffix: to cause to do.

kāsāva.meaning: the yellow robe; ochre-coloured (robe of a monk)
kāsāva.gram: adjective, used as a noun (from kasāva "astringent, ochre")
breakdown: Astringent; the ochre dye; lengthened to kāsāva, the yellow-brown robe of the renunciate.

kāsāva yellow.gram: adjective used as a noun (duplicate row; see kāsāva)
kāsāva yellow.note: uncertain: this headword is a glossary-row artifact in which the English gloss "yellow" was attached to the headword kāsāva; see the entry kāsāva.

breakdown: Ochre, yellow-brown; the colour of the renunciate's robes.

kāyaduccaritaṁ.meaning: bad bodily action; misconduct by body
breakdown: Prefix: bad, evil. (The doubling cc is junction doubling.)

kāyaduccaritādīnametaṁ.meaning: this... of bad bodily conduct and the rest
kāyaduccaritādīnametaṁ.gram: sandhi chain: compound in the genitive plural + pronoun (kāyaduccaritādīnaṁ + etaṁ)
breakdown: Genitive plural ending; the niggahīta becomes m before the vowel.

kāyaparihārikena.meaning: just enough to maintain the body; for the upkeep of the body

kāyavacīpayogasamuṭṭhāpikā.meaning: occasioned by means of body and speech; causing (kamma) through bodily and verbal effort

kāḷapakkheva.meaning: just as in the dark fortnight; like the waning half of the month
kāḷapakkheva.gram: masculine compound (locative singular) + comparison particle iva/eva, fused
breakdown: Half of the lunar month, fortnight; kāḷapakkha is the moonless, waning half.
Just as, like (fused with the locative pakkhe).
Root: to be crooked; kūṭa is what is crooked, false.

kūṭamānakūṭā.meaning: false measures and false scales; cheating with weights and measures
kūṭamānakūṭā.summary: From the stock list tulākūṭa-kaṁsakūṭa-mānakūṭa: false scales, false coinage and false measures.
breakdown: Scale, balance (in the fuller list tulākūṭa).
Bronze plate, coinage (in the fuller list kaṁsakūṭa).

la, ḷa.meaning: the letters la and ḷa, which often get swapped in Pāli words
la, ḷa.summary: A pronunciation note: when pronouncing ḷa the tongue moves from above the teeth towards the lips, performing a trembling sound.
la, ḷa.gram: phrase: phonological note on two letters of the Pāli alphabet

labbhati.meaning: to be gained; to be obtained
labbhati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, passive of labhati (√labh)

labbhetha.meaning: could be obtained; might be taken
labbhetha.gram: verb, optative (middle form) of the passive labbhati
